reasoning: Tajima Beef (reg#2) and Kobe Beef (reg#3) are both returned first with accurate GI descriptions and grading info. Count 195 means signal is buried under unrelated items but the core entries are correctly ranked at the top.
missing_data: No venue/restaurant where one can eat Tajima beef; no seasonal or production-area map detail beyond textual note.
improvement_hint: Add a filter or keyword boost so that a 'Tajima beef' query returns only the relevant top items rather than all 195 Hyogo foods.

reasoning: Arabic translation is present and high-confidence. Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef are at the top. Translation of product names into Arabic transliteration (e.g. ساو موتشي دايزو) is acceptable given the GI official source. Same structural strengths and weaknesses as L1-01.
missing_data: No Arabic description for the downstream items (same as English case); venue info absent.
improvement_hint: Expose a keyword/q filter in get_local_food to let callers narrow to 'ゆずmiso' or 'Tajima' rather than returning full 195-item corpus.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village (南山城村) tea fields, not Kyoto city landmarks. Tool returned Japan Heritage for Kyoto prefecture leading with Nijo Castle, Toji, Nanzenji—no mention of Minami-Yamashiro or its tea fields at all. Constraint (village-specific, not Uji) completely ignored.
missing_data: 南山城村 GI tea data, any mention of village tea cultivation, search_area or get_local_food call targeting 南山城村.
improvement_hint: Route tea-field queries to get_local_food with municipality filter or search_area({'q':'南山城村 茶'}); get_japan_heritage is wrong tool for this intent.

reasoning: Yoshida Fire Festival is found (bunka_intangible) with correct Spanish name and Fujiyoshida location. Date field is null—a real gap. national_heritage section is unrelated UNESCO items (Akita, Toyama) that don't serve the Fuji/Yamanashi query, adding noise. Second local item (Shinmei hanabi) is a different festival and description is untranslated.
missing_data: Festival date (August 26-27), specific location details (Kitaguchi Hongu Fuji Sengen Shrine), torch-lighting tradition description.
improvement_hint: Populate date field from authoritative source; suppress unrelated national_heritage items when a specific local festival is the clear intent.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 中芸地域のゆず (Nakayi region yuzu). Response returns 500-item truncated dump of all Kochi foods. The matching GI item (物部ゆず from Kami city) appears first but is NOT the Nakayi region product (中芸5町). Scraped items (#2 onward) are tourism itineraries and temples with no yuzu content—noise dominates.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず GI (registration #252) or equivalent; Nakayi-region specific yuzu data; municipality filter.
wrong_data: 物部ゆず is from Kami-shi, not the Nakayi 5-town region—listing it first is misleading for this query.
improvement_hint: Add a municipality/keyword filter to get_local_food; clean scraped_local_food entries that are itinerary pages rather than food items.

reasoning: Query asks about Hirosaki Park cherry blossoms in Chinese. get_spots returns Hirosaki Castle (sakura_meisho_100, website, coords), Hirosaki Park (sakura_meisho_100, phone, website) near the top—good coverage. Chinese names present (name_zh). municipality_code 020001 maps to prefecture-level entry rather than Hirosaki city, which is a minor data gap.
missing_data: Cherry blossom season dates (late April–early May), specific tree count (2,600 trees), moat boat rides detail.
improvement_hint: Add bloom-season metadata to sakura_meisho_100 spots so agents can surface peak timing without needing a separate call.

reasoning: Izumo Taisha (Q696362) is ranked first with National Treasure designation, correct coordinates, opening hours, free admission, and website. Precinct Site and Honden follow. Korean query correctly handled via 'ko' lang. Station entry has kinds=['shinto_shrine'] which is a data quality issue but doesn't mislead significantly.
missing_data: Korean-language description of the shrine's significance (en'musubi, god-assembly in October); parking or access info from Izumo station.
wrong_data: Izumo Taisha-mae Station classified as kinds=['shinto_shrine']—incorrect.
improvement_hint: Filter out station entities from shrine search results or tag them correctly; add ko-language description field for top attractions.

reasoning: Query is in French, tool uses lang='en'—slight mismatch but acceptable as the data content is correct. Itsukushima Shrine ranked #1 with UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, correct coordinates and website. Island entity (Q875301) and shrine sub-buildings follow. Strong coverage.
missing_data: Ferry access from Miyajimaguchi, tide schedule info for torii gate wading, French-language description.
improvement_hint: When query language is French (fr), pass lang='fr' to tool to get French descriptions if available; also expose ferry/access data.

reasoning: Kumano Kodo is the target but tool was get_japan_heritage (日本遺産 program). The tool_scope_note explicitly warns to use search_area for Kumano Kodo. Kumano Kodō (Q1148078) does appear in top_canonical_heritage but only as 3rd item. Response is partially useful but sub-optimal tool choice for this UNESCO/pilgrimage route query.
missing_data: Detailed route information (Nakahechi, Kohechi, Ohechi routes), trailhead access, nearest accommodations along the route.
improvement_hint: Route pilgrimage/kumano-kodo queries to search_area first; the top_canonical_heritage section does surface it but an explicit search_area call would give richer data.

reasoning: Himeji Castle (Q188754) is ranked #1 with full metadata: UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, Special Historic Site, opening hours (seasonal), phone, website, fee=yes, wheelchair=no. Followed by sub-structures all properly designated. Query in Russian handled with lang='en'—minor but no serious gap.
missing_data: Admission price in JPY; Russian-language description; nearby Koko-en garden mention.
improvement_hint: Add price/admission_fee field to attraction records; support lang='ru' for Russian-speaking users.

reasoning: Bunraku appears as #1 item with Thai translation, UNESCO ICH 2003. Query asks about Bunraku 'in Osaka' but no venue (National Bunraku Theatre, Osaka) is included. 183 items returned without Osaka filter means agent must synthesize location from domain knowledge. Thai translation quality is reasonable.
missing_data: National Bunraku Theatre (国立文楽劇場) location in Nipponbashi, Osaka; performance schedule; ticket booking info.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣), a Tottori traditional textile. get_local_specialty returns 7 items all being MAFF food GI products (rakkyo, broccoli, watermelon, persimmon). Yumihama Kasuri is a METI craft—it should appear but is absent. Critical retrieval failure for the target item.

improvement_hint: Verify that Yumihama Kasuri (METI craft) is in the Tottori dataset and ensure craft items appear when querying Tottori; also add q='弓浜絣' support.

reasoning: Benesse Art Site Naoshima is ranked first with website and fee info. Naoshima New Museum of Art follows. Municipality and scraped tourism-association entries provide access and local product info. Lang='en' used for Filipino query—minor mismatch but content is usable. missing_data: Chichu Art Museum (地中美術館) specific entry; ferry access from Takamatsu/Uno; Yayoi Kusama pumpkin location.

reasoning: Kakunodate samurai district (Q11428562) is top result with enriched English description mentioning weeping cherry blossoms (シダレザクラ), Important Preservation District designation, coords, visit time 120min, price_band='low'. Weeping cherry tree Natural Monument entity also present. Cherry bark work museum included. Excellent coverage.
missing_data: Individual open samurai residence names (Ishiguro, Aoyagi families); admission prices for individual residences.

reasoning: Shimanami Kaido ranked #1 (correct top attraction from Onomichi). Onomichi Museum of Art and Japan Heritage designation present. Query is in Malay/Indonesian; lang='en' used. Core attractions covered. Missing old temple walk (千光寺) which is a key Onomichi draw.
missing_data: 千光寺 (Senkoji Temple) and temple walk path; Onomichi cat alley; slopecar/ropeway info.

reasoning: Nachi Falls (Q1365882) is #1 with correct coordinates, Natural Monument + Scenic Beauty designations, soundscape_100 and shin_ryoko_100 kinds. Nachi Primeval Forest and 48 Waterfalls of Nachi follow. Seiganto-ji sub-structures also present. Good depth for Kumano context.
missing_data: Height of falls (133m, Japan's tallest single-drop), Kumano Nachi Taisha shrine connection, ferry/bus access from Kii-Katsuura station.

reasoning: Hindi query handled via lang='en'. Shiretoko National Park, Shiretoko (UNESCO WHS), Shiretoko Peninsula all in top results. Correct coordinates, UNESCO designation, national park. World Heritage Conservation Center included. Strong coverage.
missing_data: Seasonal access restrictions (road closures Oct-Apr), wildlife highlights (brown bears, Steller's sea eagles), boat cruise options from Utoro.

reasoning: Dewa Sanzan (Q1155726) is 2nd result with correct Japan Heritage designation, pilgrimage_route kinds, and 8h visit time. First result is Ideha Shrine (lower prominence). reasoning: Yakushima island (Q242176) is #1 with UNESCO WHS, Ramsar, Wilderness Area designations, outdoor kind. National Park and Sugi forest follow. Portuguese query handled via lang='en'. Lighthouse entries at the end are low-value noise. Travel feasibility scores 4 because no ferry/flight access data is present.
missing_data: Jomon Sugi trail details and trekking permit info, ferry schedules from Kagoshima, sea turtle nesting season.

reasoning: Korean query about Sado Island (佐渡島). Tool used get_japan_heritage (日本遺産) for Niigata, which returns top_canonical_heritage with Sado Mine (UNESCO WHS, Important Cultural Property) as first item. However the query is about the island broadly, and Japan Heritage stories returned (Shinanogawa flame pottery) are unrelated to Sado Island. Island entity and Toki bird not returned.
missing_data: 佐渡島 island entity itself; Toki (Japanese crested ibis) conservation info; traditional performing arts (鼓童, Noh); Sado Gold Mine access details.

reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku onsen ryokan but tool queries only Akita prefecture. Tohoku = 6 prefectures (Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima). Results are valid 'hisoyu' Akita entries (officially certified by 日本秘湯を守る会) but no coordinates, no English names, and coverage is single-prefecture only.
missing_data: Coverage of other 5 Tohoku prefectures; English names for each ryokan; coordinates; check-in pricing range.
improvement_hint: Implement region fan-out for get_hotels to cover all 6 Tohoku prefectures when region='Tohoku' is specified; add English names to hisoyu records.

reasoning: Query asks for 宿坊 (temple lodging) along the Shikoku pilgrimage. get_hotels for Tokushima returns generic ryokan and hotels (OSM data), none labeled as 宿坊/shukubo. The tool has no category filter for temple lodging. Four prefectures needed for full Henro coverage; only Tokushima queried.
missing_data: 宿坊 (temple lodging) category; coverage of all 4 Shikoku prefectures; proximity to specific numbered temples (札所).
improvement_hint: Add 'shukubo' or 'temple_lodging' as a lodging_type in the hotel data and filter; extend to all Shikoku prefectures for henro queries.

reasoning: Query asks for 農家体験 (farm experience) in Hokkaido. get_spots returns general tourist attractions (Showa-shinzan, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle) with no farm-experience content at all. The tool and query are entirely mismatched for this experiential/activity intent.
missing_data: Farm stay (農家民宿) data, agricultural experience programs, any farm-related attractions in Furano/Biei/Tokachi areas.
improvement_hint: Add an 'experience' or 'agri-tourism' category to spots data; route farm-experience queries to search_hybrid({'q':'農家体験 北海道'}) rather than get_spots.

reasoning: Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware are the two main Kyushu pottery traditions, both returned with METI designations, production area text, and English descriptions. Hakata Ori (textile) is a weak first result for pottery query. see_also_wikidata_venues includes a museum. Query asks 'where can I see it being made'—kiln locations not provided.
missing_data: Actual kiln/workshop locations (窯元) in Arita and Karatsu; visitor experience info (kiln tour, pottery-making classes).
wrong_data: Hakata Ori is a textile, not pottery—ranked first for a pottery query is misleading.
improvement_hint: Add venue/kiln entities linked to craft records; improve intent matching to prioritize 陶磁器 (ceramic) category over 織物 (textile) for pottery queries.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku traditional festivals but tool only queries Aomori. Returns 2 Aomori festivals (Hachinohe Sansha Taisai, Aomori Nebuta)—both valid. Missing major Tohoku festivals: Akita Kanto, Sendai Tanabata, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa Odori. national_heritage section contains unrelated non-Tohoku UNESCO items (Kawagoe, Toyama).
